title: I'm a dietitian with over 14 years of experience. Here are 9 frozen meals I buy at Trader Joe's for easy lunches.
author: Maxine Yeung
fullText:
I'm a dietitian with over 14 years of experience. Here are 9 frozen meals I buy at Trader Joe's for easy lunches.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 It was tough to narrow down which frozen meals from Trader Joe's I like best, but here are nine of my favorites.
 
 Maxine Yeung 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 As a dietitian, I like some of Trader Joe's frozen meals, which are quick to prepare for lunch. 
 Trader Joe's channa masala, palak paneer, and pad see ew taste like they're from a restaurant.
 I also buy the chain's chicken burrito bowls and frozen vegan Thai curry meals. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 I make fresh lunches for my kids every day, but when it comes to myself, I don't always have the time. So, I always keep a few of Trader Joe's frozen meals on hand. Most heat up in five minutes or less, and are far more budget-friendly than ordering takeout every day. Best of all, the chain has a wide variety of options, from Indian-inspired dishes to French classics, plus plenty of vegetarian and vegan choices.As a dietitian with over 14 years of experience, I'm pleased some of these are a decent source of protein and fiber, a nutrient most of us don't get enough of on a daily basis.You'll want to be mindful when fitting these into your diet, though, as frozen meals are typically high in sodium. Some of these have more than half of what the average adult should consume in a day. Without further ado, here are some of my favorite Trader Joe's frozen lunches. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Cod Provençale with ratatouille and rice feels like a meal you'd order at a French restaurant.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Cod Provençale comes with brown rice and stewed vegetables.
 
 Maxine Yeung 
 
 
 
 Herb-seasoned cod is paired with brown rice with a medley of stewed summer vegetables, like zucchini, eggplant, tomatoes, and bell peppers. With 23 grams of protein, 6 grams of fiber, and just 370 calories, this meal is light but satisfying. And if you're worried about microwaving fish, don't be — the fragrant ratatouille fills the room instead!
 
 
 
 
 
 
 This Asian-style spicy noodle bowl with chicken delivers on peanut flavor.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Trader Joe's has several types of frozen noodle bowls.
 
 Maxine Yeung 
 
 
 
 Ground chicken and vegetables, like bok choy, carrots, and green onions, help bring 19 grams of protein and 4 grams of fiber to this bowl.It's got a nice, nutty flavor and I've found the noodles maintain a perfect texture, even after being microwaved — they're not mushy at all. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Channa masala is a staple in my freezer.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 I serve Trader Joe's channa masala with rice or naan.
 
 Maxine Yeung 
 
 
 
 Chickpeas star in this Indian-style dish, and are stewed with tomatoes, onions, cilantro, bay leaves, and warm spices like turmeric.This is a great pick for those who love chicken tikka masala but would prefer a plant-based option.This frozen meal contains two servings, with each containing 7 grams of fiber and 6 grams of protein. For a heartier meal, serve this with Trader Joe's frozen rice or naan to help soak up all the flavorful masala. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Trader Joe's chicken shawarma bowl comes alive with the tangy, slightly sweet crunch of pickled red onions.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 I especially like the onions in Trader Joe's chicken shawarma bowl.
 
 Maxine Yeung 
 
 
 
 This meal may work for those following the Mediterranean Diet, which is often ranked as one of the most balanced ways to eat.It simply features fluffy basmati rice, shawarma seasoned chicken, red bell peppers, and spinach, all topped with a creamy garlic sauce and crunchy pickled red onions.With 20 grams of protein, the 400-calorie bowl is both flavorful and filling.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 The vegan Thai green curry is comfort food with a spicy kick.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 I enjoy Trader Joe's vegan Thai green curry.
 
 Maxine Yeung 
 
 
 
 Coconut, green chili, and lemongrass create that classic Thai balance of creamy, sweet, and spicy, with tofu and produce like eggplant, carrots, and cabbage served over jasmine rice to soak up the sauce.This cozy Trader Joe's meal provides 14 grams of plant-based protein with 7 grams of fiber in a 470-calorie serving. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Trader Joe's frozen pad see ew could easily pass for a restaurant dish.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 I've been impressed by the texture and flavor of Trader Joe's pad see ew.
 
 Maxine Yeung 
 
 
 
 This vegan, Thai-inspired meal combines wide stir-fried rice noodles, vegetables, tofu, and soy sauce. The soft noodles paired with the crunchy Chinese broccoli and carrots make this dish especially hearty and enjoyable. It contains two 300-calorie servings, with each containing 6 grams of protein and 3 grams of fiber. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 The chicken burrito bowl combines animal- and plant-based proteins with whole grains and vegetables.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Trader Joe's chicken burrito bowl is quite filling.
 
 Maxine Yeung 
 
 
 
 Seasoned chicken breast, black beans, brown rice, red quinoa, corn, cheddar, and a chipotle sauce come together to create a balanced, flavorful, restaurant-quality lunch. With 22 grams of protein and 9 grams of fiber, it's filling and satisfying enough to help get you through the day. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Trader Joe's riced cauliflower bowl works well as a vegan meal or snack.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Trader Joe's riced cauliflower bowl is vegan and gluten-free.
 
 Maxine Yeung 
 
 
 
 This simple vegan microwavable bowl has marinated chickpeas, sweet potatoes, tofu, and onions tossed in a creamy tahini sauce. Each package has about 2½ servings, each containing about 5 grams of protein and 3 grams of fiber. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Palak paneer is a high-protein, high-fiber vegetarian standout.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Trader Joe's palak paneer is vegetarian.
 
 Maxine Yeung 
 
 
 
 This classic low-carb Indian dish features paneer, a mild cheese, that soaks up a creamy spinach sauce flavored with lots of spices and a hint of chili. Paneer is a great source of protein, and this dish packs in 19 grams along with 8 grams of fiber. As a bonus, it provides 650 milligrams of calcium — about half the daily value for adults.For extra carbohydrates, pair this with Trader Joe's frozen rice, available in white, brown, or a medley of brown and red with black barley.
